text: Continuous integration (CI) is a software development practice where team members frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Every time code is added, an automated build starts, and various tests are run. This process helps catch issues early, before they become bigger problems. CI pipelines are used to automate these steps, so developers can focus on writing new features. By using CI, teams improve code quality and speed up development, because they fix bugs soon after they appear rather than discovering them much later in the process.
